title: senecio aurellus
intro: Senecio aurellus, also known as the golden groundsel or yellow ragwort, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its bright yellow daisy-like flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Senecio aurellus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote digestive health. Senecio aurellus can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible remedy for a range of health concerns.
